case_id: Cook County COVID-19 surveillance outbreak detection

pi_items.source: CDC Epi Info 7 official documentation - canvas files use .cvs7 extension, not .canvas
Web search of Cook County public data sources and CDC data portals - no public dataset found with this exact name
CDC Epi Info 7 User Guide - Visual Dashboard includes Aberration Detection Analysis gadget for EARS implementation
CDC research on EARS methods and Epi Info 7 implementation - C2-MEDIUM is the correct formal name, using CUSUM-based calculation with 2-day temporal guard band
Reasonable field name for time series data in disease surveillance context
Standard field name used in COVID-19 surveillance datasets; matches CDC naming conventions

pi_items.verified_value: C2 is based on CUSUM but is more accurately 'C2-MEDIUM', not just a generic Cumulative Sum method
pi_items.source: CDC research and EARS technical documentation - C2 implements a specific CUSUM-based algorithm with guards and baselines, not generic cumulative sum

privileged_info_summary: The task implements outbreak detection using Epi Info 7's Aberration Detection Analysis gadget with the verified C2-MEDIUM method (EARS), which is a real CDC surveillance tool. The dataset 'cook_county_daily.csv' is synthetic and not available in public CDC/Cook County data sources. The canvas file extension should be .cvs7, not .canvas as claimed in metadata. The task operates on Cook County COVID-19 case data with 'date' and 'cases' fields for aberration detection using a 7-day baseline with 2-day guard band.
